column2: The private IPv4 block beginning with 10 is {{c1::10.0.0.0/8}}.
column3: This is the largest of the three private IPv4 blocks.
column4:
Memory Trick

The entire first octet is fixed at 10 because the prefix length is /8.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.17; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.6
column9: A+::220-1201::2.6 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::BasicSOHONetworkConfiguration Cloze HighYield IPv4 PrivateAddressing Source::Messer-v170

column2: The private IPv4 block covering 172.16.0.0 through 172.31.255.255 is {{c1::172.16.0.0/12}}.
column3: The second octet runs from 16 through 31 inside this private block.
column4:
Exam Trap

Not every address beginning with 172 is private; the private range is limited to this /12 block.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.17; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.6
column9: A+::220-1201::2.6 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::BasicSOHONetworkConfiguration Cloze HighYield IPv4 PrivateAddressing Source::Messer-v170

column2: The private IPv4 block beginning with 192.168 is {{c1::192.168.0.0/16}}.
column3: Addresses in this block are common on SOHO networks.
column4:
Memory Trick

The /16 prefix fixes the first two octets: 192.168.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.17; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.6
column9: A+::220-1201::2.6 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::BasicSOHONetworkConfiguration Cloze HighYield IPv4 PrivateAddressing Source::Messer-v170

column3: The approved source identifies 169.254.1.0 through 169.254.254.255 as the functional assignment range.
column4:
Exam Trap

Routers do not forward APIPA traffic, so this block supports local-link communication rather than normal internet access.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.18; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.6

column3: APIPA supplies an IPv4 link-local address when DHCP configuration is unavailable.
column4:
Exam Trap

Despite the word “Private,” APIPA is specifically link-local and is not forwarded by routers.
